title: Views of Whitney
summary:
Driving into Alabma Hills felt kind of magical. It was our first time being back in the mountains in a while and I didn't realize how much I missed it until we were there. The free camping here is some of the nicest camping we have found and I couldn't quite believe it was free. The view out of our window was big open sage fields and rolling rock piles set against the still snowy Eastern Sierras. The first afternoon that we arrived, we walked over to one of the big rock piles and did a couple of climbing routes. The next day, we drove further up into the mountains to the Whitney Portal where many of the trails to Mt Whitney start. It was definitely chilly up there, with snow still on the ground but it was very sunny and we soon were stripped down to t-shirts. We made it up to Lone Pine Lake, only getting lost in the snow fields at the top a couple of times. Maya was excited to roll in the snow and we were excited to glasade down some of the route. The following day, we spent exploring the area around our campsite, Colin running while I biked along beside him. This area is famous for being the sets of many movies, hence the name Movie Flats. The cactus were blooming, butterflies were flying all around and it was one of those days where I felt in awe that this is our life right now. Our friend Abby met up with us that night and we set up the projector for the first of many movie nights in the van.

-Helen
